concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains the Great Seal of the State of North Carolina prominently at the top of the first page. In the HTML output, this meaningful organizational seal is missing as an image element and is instead incorrectly rendered as a plain text placeholder.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Great Seal of the State of North Carolina image at the top of the page.
concerns.output_quote: [Image: Great Seal of the State of North Carolina]

concerns.description: Fabricated paragraph (0 source overlap): "NORTH CAROLINA ***Environmental Quality***"

concerns.analysis: The HTML output contains the full text extraction for the second page (WebEx Meeting Information), but then immediately includes a large image that represents the entirety of that same document page. This causes the user to see the page content twice: once as structured HTML text and once embedded in the page screenshot image.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 2 text content starting with 'WebEx Meeting Information:'
concerns.output_quote:
WebEx Meeting Information:

...

Access code: 243 537 31319

\n
concerns.location: Page 2 content at the end of the HTML

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 1.5x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=1.50. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.

concerns.description: List numbering restart(s): 1 ordered list(s) restart numbering at 1 instead of continuing sequence
concerns.analysis: restart_count=1. Examples: p1: 2 single-item ordered lists (fragmented). Ordered lists that restart numbering suggest content was split across page breaks and not reassembled.

The source document underlines the text 'Action Item:' and 'Informational Items:' on page 1. The output JSON preserves the italics using markdown but does not capture the underline, constituting a loss of visual emphasis. (Note: Underline is also lost on page 2 for several headings, but they were converted to HTML structural headings, which is an acceptable transformation. However, the items on page 1 are standard paragraphs that lost underline.)

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 1 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=1, examples=['https://ncgov.webex.com/ncgov/j.php?MTID=m626ebfe839fa9b0c8a']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality
217 West
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
